metadata.title:
The Toll of Blood
That's in the Coal

pages.raw_text:
Are you and doing our part? All of us use COAL What
Is our obligation?


their greed and disregard of human life which are re-
sponslbe for these frequent horrors is the wordod
habit to see the fault of others rather than own


THESE miners lives have been sacrificed in a social
service In truth they have been working for every one
OF us that use coal Have responsibility for con-
dtons in the mines? Are the widows and orphans alone
70 carry the great loss by the death of husbands fathers
sbreadwinners every of them? We cannot bind


up the broken hearts fill the vacant chairs in the home
birce. We can, though, be husband in doing justice


we can reduce the number of such horrors This is not
an appeal for aims or charity; only for JUSTICE
Ind nothing


I Some measure of justice can be attained by making


mine operators liable for those killed injured in their
kervce For every miner killed certain sums fixed by
law should be paid to those dependent upon his labor
proportioned to his injury These payments should not
depend upon fault of except the person killed
injured.


The present system of liability depends upon neg
gence. If you cannot prove that the mine owners
negligent they not liable If miner careless
all the rest, matter how careful they had been
equally responsible. The whole negligence theory is
wrong 1t imposes upon those least able to bear it the
Rhole burden in the great majority of Society
you and 1-are unjust.


By making compensation in case of accident
pense of the business just much wages paid not only
mine owners, but coal users help to bear the burden
Thus it is distributed and the present injustice is m
gated.


But the important consequences is that accidents
would become less frequent. This is the experience of
countries that have tried the system The careless and
Inefficient mine is put out of business. Freedom
from accident ability to produce coal at lower
cost Ingenuity and business ability directed to pre-


venting accidents The sensitive of the pOcketbOOk
Is touched


Is it not time that America arousedi In no other
rountry the world is the disregard of human life


great The annual sacrifice of lives in mines appal
ing. Our system wrong The responsibility of every
man and woman must be made clear. Public opinion
must be awakened Then the system will be changed.
Legislation will compel greater Of justice and
these horrors will become less frequent
